compiled_state.title: As a developer or agent, I can delete a ticket using ticket delete --user-approved, which marks it deleted and removes it from active views
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: New `ticket-delete.sh` script implementing the full local deletion operation.
**Why**: Provides the core mechanism for permanently removing tickets from the event-sourced store, which is a prerequisite for all other epic stories and directly addresses the user problem of having no deletion path.
**Scope**:
- IN: `--user-approved` gate (exits non-zero with instructional error if flag absent; flag absent from help text); non-deleted-children guard (exits non-zero listing blocking children); atomic git commit writing STATUS(deleted) + ARCHIVED + UNLINK events (for all depends_on links where the deleted ticket is the blocking target); `ticket list` exclusion via existing ARCHIVED filtering; `ticket show <id>` returns status:deleted and archived:true; ticket-graph.py terminal status check updated to treat 'deleted' equivalently to 'closed'
- OUT: Jira propagation (Story 3), `ticket transition` blocking (Story 2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `ticket delete <id>` without `--user-approved` exits non-zero with an instructional error message directing the agent to confirm with the user and re-run with `--user-approved`; the flag is absent from help text
  ← Satisfies: "`ticket delete <id>` without `--user-approved` exits non-zero with an instructional error message"
- When this story is complete, `ticket delete <id> --user-approved` exits non-zero listing any child tickets not in `deleted` status
  ← Satisfies: "`ticket delete <id> --user-approved` exits non-zero with a clear error listing any child tickets not in `deleted` status"
- When this story is complete, `ticket delete <id> --user-approved` on a valid ticket atomically writes STATUS(deleted) + ARCHIVED + UNLINK events (for all depends_on links where this ticket is the blocking target) in a single git commit
  ← Satisfies: "atomically writes a STATUS(deleted) event and an ARCHIVED event in a single git commit" and "All depends_on links where the deleted ticket is the blocking target are removed via UNLINK events"
- When this story is complete, `ticket list` (default output) excludes the deleted ticket; `ticket show <id>` returns status:deleted and archived:true
  ← Satisfies: "After deletion, ticket list (default output) excludes the deleted ticket; ticket show still works"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Consumer Impact] ticket-graph.py ready_to_work only recognizes 'closed' as terminal; add 'deleted' to ticket-graph.py's terminal status check so that deleted tickets are not treated as open blockers for dependents
- [Reliability] UNLINK scope must explicitly cover: (1) inbound depends_on links where deleted ticket is the target/blocker, (2) source-side depends_on links where deleted ticket is the source (these become irrelevant; document whether they are cleaned up or left), (3) clarify whether relates_to reciprocal links in other ticket directories are cleaned up
- [Reliability] Use fcntl.flock consistent with existing ticket-transition.sh locking to minimize the guard-check/commit window for concurrent modifications
